title: viburnum hyemale
intro: Viburnum hyemale, also known as the witherod or snow bum, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be utilized to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Viburnum hyemale have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in salves and creams to topically apply its medicinal properties.
